data.content:
Use `&` to combine conditions in the `--filter` expression:

```bash
dotnet test --filter "ClassName~OrderServiceTests & TestCategory=Unit"
```

This uses:
- `ClassName~OrderServiceTests` — matches the class name (`~` means "contains")
- `&` — AND operator
- `TestCategory=Unit` — matches the `[TestCategory("Unit")]` attribute

This will run only `CreateOrder_ValidInput_ReturnsOrder` and `CreateOrder_NullInput_ThrowsException`, skipping the Integration tests and everything in `PaymentServiceTests`.
data.reasoningText: The user wants to filter tests by both class (`OrderServiceTests`) and category (`Unit`). This is an MSTest project using VSTest. They need to combine two filter conditions with `&` (AND operator) in the `--filter` expression.
